data.title: TXNM Energy Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 in-line
data.description:
• TXNM Energy press release (NYSE:TXNM): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 in-line.
  • 2025 ongoing earnings guidance range introduced at $2.74 - $2.84 per diluted share reflects strong Texas growth, mid-year implementation of PNM customer rate phase-in
  • Increased long-term earnings per share growth target of 7% to 9%

data.title: Clear Secure declares $0.125 dividend, $0.27 special dividend
data.description:
• Clear Secure (NYSE:YOU) declares $0.125/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous, and a special cash dividend of $0.27/share.
  • Both dividends are payable March 18; for shareholders of record March 10; ex-div March 10.
  • In addition, the company announced that its board authorized a $200M increase to its existing Class A common stock repurchase program, resulting in an aggregate remaining authorization of ~$232M after using ~$68M subsequent to Q324.
  • See YOU Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: AdvanSix Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09, revenue of $329M misses by $48.15M
data.description:
• AdvanSix press release (NYSE:ASIX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.09.
  • Revenue of $329M (-13.9% Y/Y) misses by $48.15M.
  • Sales down approximately 14% versus prior year driven by an approximately 16% decrease in volume primarily as a result of the extended plant turnaround, partially offset by 2% favorable impact of market-based pricing

data.title: Emera Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.84; reaffirms lon-term outlook
data.description:
• Emera press release (OTCPK:EMRAF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$0.84.

  • Outlook: Emera remains committed to achieving its targeted 5-7% average adjusted EPS growth through 2027 and to deliver on its C$20 billion 5-year capital plan. These customer-focused investments will drive a forecasted 7-8% rate base growth.

data.title: Rivian to recall 17,260 vehicles in U.S. due to issue with headlight, shares drop
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) is recalling 17,260 vehicles in the U.S. due to a headlight issue that can reduce visibility, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Friday.

The company’s shares slipped about 6% to $12.80 in premarket trading.

"The recall population includes certain Model Year 2025 R1T and R1S vehicles thatmay have been built with headlamps that do not meet current productionspecifications due to incorrectly configured parts from the supplier," the auto safety regulator said in a notice.

This issue can only occur when starting the vehicle in cold temperatures and a message that says “Low beam lights not working. Service low beams soon” will appear on the driver display, alerting the driver that their lights are not illuminated, the notice read.

The company will replace the headlight control modules without charge to fix the issue, according to the regulator's report.

Rivian Automotive (NASDAQ:RIVN) on Thursday set 2025 delivery guidance at 46,000 to 51,000 vehicles.

data.title: OpenAI launches Operator—an AI agent to handle tasks for human
data.description:
Microsoft-backed (NASDAQ:MSFT) OpenAI rolled out its AI agent—Operator—to Pro users in Australia, Brazil, Canada, India, Japan, Singapore, South Korea, the UK, and more countries. It will be accessible in most places where ChatGPT is available, except the EU, Switzerland, Norway, Liechtenstein, and Iceland.

Operator is designed to automate various web-based tasks for users, including booking tickets, making restaurant reservations, filling out online forms, shopping on e-commerce platforms, among others.

The tool operates through a dedicated web browser interface that users can control at any time. It utilizes a computer-using agent model, which allows it to interact with graphical user interfaces on websites similarly to how a human would.

The AI agent will be exclusively available to subscribers of the $200-per-month ChatGPT Pro plan. This launch follows its initial introduction in the U.S. in January.

OpenAI claims that Operator outperforms similar rival tools, including Anthropic’s Computer Use and Google (GOOG) DeepMind’s Mariner.

The company said that it is working to make the tool available in more regions, including the EU.

data.title: Chinese tech stocks rally on AI optimism, robust earnings
data.description:
China’s tech stocks rallied on Friday, as sentiment was helped by robust earnings reports from several companies and growing optimism for artificial intelligence-driven technologies.

The Hang Seng Tech Index, a Hong Kong index that tracks Chinese tech stocks, closed at 6.5%, touching its highest levels since early 2022, as per a Bloomberg report.

The index was boosted by favorable earnings reports from firms like Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) as it beat its Q3 estimates.

Other companies like Lenovo (OTCPK:LNVGY) and Xiaomi (OTCPK:XIACF) also contributed heavily to the index. The former closed at 15.5% as it hovered around a ten-year-high, while the latter was up 5.2%, touching a record high.

China’s tech stocks are reaping the benefits of the country’s advancement in artificial intelligence.

The optimism comes as DeepSeek, a Chinese AI app, managed to rattle Silicon Valley and as new possibilities open for Chinese tech companies.

DeepSeek gained global attention after it recently released its open-source AI model, DeepSeek-R1.

China-linked ETFs: (KWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:FLCH), (BATS:CNYA), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), and (NYSEARCA:YINN).

data.title: NaaS Technology sets $10M stock buyback plan
data.description:
• NaaS Technology (NASDAQ:NAAS) has approved a share repurchase program to purchase up to US$10 million of its American depositary shares over the next 12 months through the end of February 2026.
  • The board will review the share repurchase program periodically, and may authorize adjustment of its terms and size.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures rise after sell-off triggered by Walmart's outlook
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed higher on Friday, as markets look to regain some momentum after Thursday's sell-off, which was partly driven by Walmart's disappointing outlook.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.50%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.27%.

U.S. stocks finished with losses Thursday, with the S&P 500 pulling back from a record high, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

"We saw a moderate risk-off move yesterday, with the S&P 500 falling back from its all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The main story in the U.S. was a weaker-than-expected forecast from Walmart, which added to nerves about the health of the consumer right now, especially after a soft retail sales print last week, Reid added.

On the economic side, the February PMI composite flash and January existing home sales are due during market hours. The latter is expected to come in at 4.13M.

The February consumer sentiment is forecasted to come in at 67.8.

"Final February U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment data is mainly of use as a source of entertainment, such is the degree of political partisanship," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

However, approval for U.S. President Donald Trump’s economic policies and Trump mega-donor Musk has been falling, and the Michigan sentiment may hint at whether that disapproval is partisan or not, Donovan added.
